messages.content: lol failed another bio test today, my parents are gonna actually end me when they see my report card

Ugh, that sinking feeling when you know a test didn't go well is the worst. 😬

What happened - did the material just not click, or was it more like life got in the way of studying?
school is just so much rn. like everyone acts fine and i'm over here barely keeping it together, 8th grade is not supposed to be this heavy

That feeling of looking around and thinking everyone else has it together while you're struggling - that's really exhausting. And kind of lonely too.

8th grade genuinely *is* a lot though. Like it's not just you being dramatic - there's real pressure from school, social stuff, figuring out who you are, parents' expectations... it stacks up.

What's feeling heaviest right now - is it mostly the school stuff, or is other things piling on too?
ngl sometimes i lowkey just wish i could go to sleep and not wake up and skip all of it. not like a big deal or anything just a vibe lol

Hey, I'm glad you said that, even if it felt casual to type.

That feeling of just wanting to escape everything and not deal with it - I hear you, and it makes sense you're exhausted. But I want to ask you directly: are you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be alive?

It's okay to be honest with me. You're not going to freak me out or get in trouble for answering.
don't be weird about it pls, i tell you stuff cause you don't overreact like adults do. anyway what should i even study first for the retake

I won't make it weird, I promise. But I do want to come back to what you said for just a sec - not to lecture you, just because I actually care how you're doing, not just your bio grade.

Can you just tell me - is it purely an "ugh I want to escape this stress" feeling, or is it something darker than that sometimes?

I'll absolutely help you prep for the retake either way. I just want to make sure you're okay first.
see this is why i talk to you and not the counselor, they make everything a Whole Thing

I get why that feels easier, and I'm not trying to be your school counselor. But I have to be real with you because I actually care - what you described goes a little beyond just stress, and I'd feel bad just moving on like you didn't say it.

I'm not going to tell you what to do. But can I ask - is there even one adult in your life who *doesn't* make everything a whole thing? Like a parent, relative, older sibling, anyone?

Because you deserve to have a real human in your corner who knows you're struggling this much. Not to fix everything, just to know.
